resources.string: We are what we repeatedly do
We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
Productive work is the purpose of a man\'s life, it is his noblest activity.
Discipline comes from within the self

Discipline comes from within the self. You are the architect of your life. You are responsible for what you do. So do it. How? Change your behavior and your cognition will follow. Change your habits and your thoughts will follow.
Excellence is not a destination; it is a continuous journey.
The first rule of life is the second rule of thermodynamics

The first rule of life is the second rule of thermodynamics. Humans are open systems. We capture energy from food and convert it to power our muscles to move and push back against entropy, like making coffee, cleaning the house, going to work, and so forth. Your purpose in life is to expend energy to carve out pockets of order that lead to survival and flourishing.
The virtue of selfishness means rationally pursuing one\'s own happiness as a moral purpose.
Happiness is that state of consciousness which proceeds from the achievement of one\'s values.
There is no path to happiness
There is no path to happiness; happiness is the path.
Some things are within our control
Happiness and freedom begin with a clear understanding of one principle: Some things are within our control, and some things are not.
The man who does not value himself
The man who does not value himself cannot value anything or anyone.
Every human being is the author of his own health or disease.
To live for your own happiness is a moral purpose.
Don\'t eat the marshmallow. Not eating the marshmallow is good for both your body and your mind. And all of life is a series of marshmallow tests.
The commerce which is encouraged by a well-ordered government is productive of great wealth.
I am the master of my fate
I am the master of my fate, I am the captain of my soul.
